steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The researcher claims some lakes have seen an increase in ice duration. The example must name a lake that had more ice days in 2005–06 than in 1980–81 — that is what "increase in duration" means.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Lake Mendota and Kalmarinjärvi, more days in 2005–06': The graph shows both of those lakes had fewer ice days in 2005–06 than in 1980–81, so this contradicts the data. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Lake Baikal, more days in 2005–06 than in 1980–81': The graph shows Lake Baikal increased from 1980–81 to 2005–06, directly supporting the researcher's claim. ✓
  • Choice C — 'Lake Baikal and Kalmarinjärvi, fewer than 200 days in 1980–81': Citing a single-year value does not demonstrate an increase over time. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Lake Baikal and Kalmarinjärvi, more than 95 days in 2005–06': Again, a single-period value cannot show whether duration increased or decreased. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
